title: GitHub Copilot
abstract: AI-powered coding assistant deployed as the first GenAI solution delivered to developers across the organization.
why_we_care: Developers need intelligent code assistance to accelerate delivery, reduce boilerplate, and improve code quality. GitHub Copilot was the first GenAI tool we evaluated and rolled out at scale, establishing the foundation for our broader AI-assisted development strategy.
summary: GitHub Copilot was the first GenAI coding assistant we delivered to our developer population. After evaluation and piloting, it was deployed broadly as an IDE-integrated tool for code completion, chat-based assistance, and code generation. It is now part of the standard developer toolkit and this initiative is archived as delivered.
description:
GitHub Copilot is an AI-powered coding assistant developed by GitHub and OpenAI. It integrates directly into IDEs (VS Code, JetBrains, etc.) and provides:

- **Inline code completion** — context-aware suggestions as you type
- **Chat interface** — ask questions, explain code, generate tests
- **Code generation** — turn natural language descriptions into working code
- **Multi-language support** — works across all major programming languages

This was our **first GenAI initiative to go live**. The journey went through:

- **Ideation** — identifying the need for AI-assisted coding
- **POC** — piloting with early adopter teams
- **POV** — measuring productivity impact and developer satisfaction
- **Go Live** — broad rollout to many users across the organization

The initiative is now **archived** as it has been successfully delivered and is part of the standard tooling. Lessons learned from this rollout informed subsequent GenAI initiatives like Claude Code evaluation and Kiro evaluation.
